title: Light and Heat
description: Explores what it means to be a source of light by examining images and building a list of natural and artificial light sources.

title: Machines and the Environment
description: Introduces the concept of "work" in machinery and how machines make moving objects easier and require less effort.

title: Mining for Batteries?
description: Examines how batteries provide portable electricity and brainstorms the many everyday products and devices that rely on them.

title: Wheels in Motion
description: Explores common devices such as wheelbarrows, screwdrivers, and doorknobs to understand wheel-and-axle simple machines.

title: Properties of Sound
description: Investigates sound as a form of energy made of vibrations that can travel through air, water, and solids in everyday life.

title: Pulley and Gear Systems
description: Explores gears and pulleys as simple machines that make work easier and identifies gear and pulley systems seen in daily life.

title: Canoes: Helping to Protect Natural Habitats
description: Examines how technology is made from natural materials and how simple technologies like canoes can help protect natural habitats.

title: Biosphere Reserve
description: Explains what a biosphere is and how life exists across the Earth, from the deepest oceans to the tallest mountains.

title: Green Iglu
description: Reflects on where fruits and vegetables come from and whether they can be grown in Canada, exploring food production challenges.

title: Code It! Recycle Bot
description: Uses coding concepts to program a robot called Recycle-Bot with movement commands, comparing and revising lines of code.

title: Save the Salmon
description: Introduces food chains by examining how energy passes from producers like grass to consumers such as grasshoppers and beyond.

title: Classifying Animals
description: Explores what different groups of animals have in common as a way of classifying them by shared characteristics.

title: Magnificent Machines
description: Examines examples of simple machines and how different types of machines make life easier and can create positive change.

title: Glow and Grow
description: Explores rainbows and how light is made of different layers of colour, prompting students to notice and wonder about light.

title: Everyday Rocks and Minerals
description: Looks at where people use rocks and minerals in their daily lives by matching pictures to descriptions of common uses.

title: Aggregate Extraction
description: Examines how a natural landscape changes through the extraction of rocks and minerals by comparing before-and-after scenes.

title: Hearing Aids
description: Introduces what it means to be Deaf or hearing-impaired through a video about a Deaf family and the ways they communicate.

title: Mechanisms and Motion
description: Explores objects that spin or rotate as examples of rotary motion and identifies similar mechanisms in everyday life.

title: Star Stories
description: Encourages students to observe a star-filled night sky and record what they know and wonder about stars.

title: Code It! Interconnections
description: Uses coding concepts while learning about invasive species, such as the Rusty Crayfish, and their effect on Ontario habitats and communities.

title: The Green Belt Movement
description: Explores the importance of forests to people and what students already know about forest habitats through a sorting activity.

title: Habitats and Animal Diets
description: Examines what a habitat is by exploring animals and plants and the living and non-living factors that meet their basic needs.

title: Food Chain Communities
description: Explores different meanings of connection, including how humans and living things feel connected to the natural environment and community.

title: Build a Beak
description: Compares animal adaptations, such as why polar bears have white fur, to understand how physical traits help animals survive.

title: Animals by Design
description: Examines how machines are part of everyday life and how all machines, complex or simple, are designed to reduce effort.

title: Light Up Your Life
description: Explores where light comes from and how natural habitats and people rely on light as a source of energy for food and growth.

title: Code It! – Invasive Species Quiz
description: Explains what an invasive species is and how introduced species disturb the ecosystems they enter, using coding concepts.

title: The Impact of Technology on Nature
description: Examines technology that produces lights and sounds, such as fireworks displays, and prompts reflection on its impact.

title: Rainforests and Deforestation
description: Explores the importance of rainforests by examining a rainforest habitat full of diverse plants and animals.

title: Protecting and Respecting the Environment
description: Explores protected natural environments around the world, such as Kruger National Park, and the importance of protecting habitats.

title: Nature is Dependent for its Survival
description: Explores how people and living things depend on one another for support, using a busy school day as a relatable example.

title: Cycles of Survival
description: Highlights Ontario's rocks and minerals and how they are used to build roads and make products like cars, wire, and batteries.

title: Food Chains?
description: Explores where food comes from by sequencing pictures into a logical order, with a focus on Ontario people, places, and innovations.

title: Rocks and Minerals
description: Uses a spot-the-differences puzzle to show how senses like sight, smell, and touch help geologists examine rocks and minerals.

title: Mighty Machines
description: Examines how machines, from simple to complex, are an important part of people's lives and help them lift and move things.

title: Mining and Nature
description: Explores Ontario's multi-million-dollar mining industry, including how mines create jobs and how minerals are found everywhere.

title: Save the Wetlands
description: Introduces Ontario's wetlands and the four types—marshes, swamps, fens, and bogs—as an important part of the province's ecosystems.

title: Sources of Light
description: Explores artificial and natural light as a type of energy and how people interact with different light sources every day.

title: Sounds Interesting
description: Examines sound as a form of energy that is all around us, from people speaking to trucks rumbling down the street.

title: Code It! Food Chain Frenzy!
description: Uses coding concepts while exploring the Great Lakes of North America, including Lake Ontario and the city of Toronto on its shore.
